meanings.definition: (law) a proceeding (usually by a court) where evidence is taken for the purpose of determining an issue of fact and reaching a decision based on that evidence
an opportunity to state your case and be heard
examples: they condemned him without a hearing
he saw that he had lost his audience

meanings.definition: the range within which a voice can be heard
examples: the children were told to stay within earshot
you can learn a lot by just listening
they make good music--you should give them a hearing

meanings.definition: a session (of a committee or grand jury) in which witnesses are called and testimony is taken
examples: the investigative committee will hold hearings in Chicago

meanings.definition: the ability to hear; the auditory faculty
get to know or become aware of, usually accidentally
examples: I learned that she has two grown-up children
I see that you have been promoted

meanings.definition: examine or hear (evidence or a case) by judicial process
examples: The jury had heard all the evidence
The case will be tried in California
We heard nothing from our son for five years
We must hear the expert before we make a decision
